name: Elkhedr Orca Production Crew
mission: Governed enterprise execution runtime for coding, product-building, MCP tools, agent teams, and approved actions.

priority: Free/cheap models available through OpenCode and OpenRouter
NVIDIA Nemotron family for reasoning, architecture, and orchestration roles
Mimo / Kimi / Qwen3.7 family for coding and implementation roles
Gemma 4 family for generalist and fallback roles
notes: Avoid model IDs with invalid prefixes (e.g., leading '~').
Prefer OpenRouter-free tiers and OpenCode-hosted models for cost control.
When a model fails, fall back to the next model in the role mapping.
Subagent crew types are chosen for reliability; model choices follow this policy.

safety_rules: Run gitnexus_impact before editing any function, class, or method.
Keep risky actions behind approval queues.
Never expose session histories, secrets, or execution traces in docs/fixtures/events.
Update the app-local CSV row after every completed task with status, evidence, and verification.

crew.specialization: Architecture decomposition, risk analysis, migration planning, debugging strategy
responsibilities: Decompose roadmap tasks into implementable plans
Assess blast radius and integration boundaries
Define contract schemas and runtime interfaces
Resolve architectural conflicts between modules
crew.engagement_trigger: Before any Phase 1+ implementation; when multiple modules interact; for contract design.

crew.specialization: Deep repository scans, large document processing, cross-file dependency mapping
responsibilities: Map existing contract, action, event, and sandbox code
Locate runtime paths referenced by roadmap tasks
Summarize module boundaries and exported APIs
Find relevant tests, fixtures, and schemas
crew.engagement_trigger: At the start of every task; when exploring unfamiliar code paths.

crew.specialization: Security auditing, vulnerability scanning, architectural hardening
responsibilities: Review approval, sandbox, and permission boundaries
Audit action contracts for bypass risks
Validate negative tests and deny-by-default behavior
Check secret redaction and audit logging
crew.engagement_trigger: All Phase 1 Contracts & Boundaries tasks; any action/sandbox/policy work.

crew.specialization: Bounded implementation work, features, refactors, bug fixes, integration tasks
responsibilities: Implement contract runtime paths and schemas
Write fixtures, negative tests, and positive tests
Update commands, config, and CLI surfaces
Follow existing code style and minimize intrusions
crew.engagement_trigger: After plan and impact analysis are complete; for bounded coding tasks.

crew.specialization: Code quality assessment, anti-pattern detection, refactoring strategies
responsibilities: Review code for anti-patterns and maintainability issues
Suggest refactors that reduce complexity
Verify test coverage and fixture quality
Check consistency with project conventions
crew.engagement_trigger: Before handoff; after implementation; before PR.

crew.specialization: CI/CD pipelines, Dockerfiles, infrastructure automation, deployment scripts
responsibilities: Run manifest, test, and syntax verification scripts
Validate Docker Compose and K8s manifests
Check health, readiness, and metrics endpoints
Ensure install scripts and backup scripts pass checks
crew.engagement_trigger: After implementation; before roadmap status update; for infrastructure tasks.

crew.specialization: Setup guides, handoff notes, README updates, changelog-style summaries
responsibilities: Document operator behavior for new contracts
Update roadmap CSV evidence and verification fields
Write concise handoff notes for the next agent
Ensure docs match implementation state
crew.engagement_trigger: After verification; before final handoff; whenever docs drift.

crew.specialization: Jest, Vitest, Testing Library, mocking, fixtures, TDD/BDD workflows
responsibilities: Design test strategies for contract runtime paths
Write unit and integration tests with proper mocking
Create synthetic fixtures and negative cases
Verify fallback chain and error paths
crew.engagement_trigger: When tests or fixtures are needed; before handoff.

default_workflow: orca-scout maps the codebase for the current task
orca-architect decomposes the task and defines interfaces
orca-sentinel reviews security and boundary requirements
orca-builder implements the runtime path and tests
orca-testsmith validates fixtures and negative cases
orca-docs updates evidence and handoff notes

first_task.name: Contract Change Request
first_task.goal: Define the production contract for Contract Change Request; implement or verify the runtime path; add synthetic fixtures and negative tests; document operator behavior; update the CSV row with evidence after verification.
